description: LAN self-hosted server (no auth, mDNS discovery, MenuBarExtra host app) and Bluetooth RFCOMM peer-to-peer mode for macOS and Windows clients

userStories.title: Server: Remove Bearer Token auth middleware by default
userStories.description: As a LAN user, I don't want to configure tokens because the server is only reachable on the home network.
acceptanceCriteria: middleware/auth.go Bearer auth middleware is preserved but disabled by default
New env var AUTH_ENABLED (default false); when true, Token auth is restored
AUTH_ENABLED=false means all /api/v1/* routes skip Authorization header check
Client does not send Authorization header when server does not require auth
go test ./... passes

userStories.title: Server: mDNS/Bonjour service broadcast
userStories.description: As a client, I want to auto-discover the CopyEverywhere server on the LAN without manually entering IP and port.
acceptanceCriteria: Server registers mDNS service on startup, type _copyeverywhere._tcp, port matches actual listen port
TXT record contains version=<server_version> and auth=<true|false>
Uses a pure-Go mDNS library (no CGO dependency)
mDNS record is deregistered on server shutdown
dns-sd -B _copyeverywhere._tcp discovers the service on the same LAN
go test ./... passes

userStories.title: Server: MenuBarExtra host app scaffold and Go subprocess management
userStories.description: As a Mac Mini user, I want the server to run as a MenuBarExtra app that manages the Go server binary as a child process.
acceptanceCriteria: New macos/CopyEverywhereServer/ SPM project, macOS 13+ MenuBarExtra app
Menu bar icon shows server status: running (green) / stopped (gray)
Go server binary is compiled independently; Swift app launches it via Process
Process stdout/stderr captured via Pipe for log display
App uses NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory) to hide Dock icon
Start/Stop button controls the Go subprocess lifecycle (launch, SIGTERM, restart)
swift build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-037 — scaffold + process management only

userStories.title: Server: MenuBarExtra config panel and status display
userStories.description: As a Mac Mini user, I want the MenuBarExtra panel to show server status and let me configure port, storage path, and TTL.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking menu bar icon opens a panel showing: listen address/port, connected device count, storage path, used space
Panel provides config fields: port (default 8080), storage path (default ~/Library/Application Support/CopyEverywhereServer/data), TTL hours
Panel shows recent log lines captured from the Go subprocess
Config changes are persisted and applied on next server restart
swift build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-037 — config panel and status display

userStories.title: Server: MenuBarExtra launch-at-login support
userStories.description: As a Mac Mini user, I want the server app to optionally start at login.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel provides a 'Launch at Login' toggle
Toggle uses SMAppService or Login Items to register/unregister
Setting is persisted across app restarts
swift build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-037 — launch-at-login only

userStories.title: Server: BIND_ADDRESS environment variable
userStories.description: As a user, I want the server to only listen on LAN interfaces, not expose to the internet.
acceptanceCriteria: New env var BIND_ADDRESS (default 0.0.0.0); MenuBarExtra config panel can set it
Server startup log prints the actual listen address
go test ./... passes

userStories.title: macOS client: mDNS auto-discovery of server
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want the client to auto-discover the CopyEverywhere server on my LAN without manual config.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses NWBrowser (Network.framework) to browse _copyeverywhere._tcp services
Discovered service IP and port are resolved and written to ConfigStore
ConfigView shows 'Discovered Servers' list; user clicks to select
Manual server address input is preserved as fallback
When server TXT auth=true, Token input field is shown; auth=false hides it
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows client: mDNS auto-discovery of server
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want the client to auto-discover the CopyEverywhere server on my LAN.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses Makaretu.Dns.Multicast or similar .NET mDNS library to browse _copyeverywhere._tcp
Discovered service IP and port written to ConfigStore
Config UI shows 'Discovered Servers' list; user clicks to select
Manual server address input preserved as fallback
When server TXT auth=true, Token input shown; auth=false hides it
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: Bluetooth RFCOMM service foundation (SDP + server/client connection)
userStories.description: As a macOS developer, I need a BluetoothService class that can publish an RFCOMM SDP record, accept inbound connections, and connect to a remote RFCOMM service.
acceptanceCriteria: New BluetoothService class wrapping IOBluetoothRFCOMMChannel
Defines CopyEverywhere RFCOMM Service UUID (CE000001-...), shared across platforms
Server mode: publishes SDP service record, waits for inbound RFCOMM connections
Client mode: SDP query discovers peer CopyEverywhere service, establishes RFCOMM connection
swift build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-041 — connection layer only, no data protocol yet

userStories.title: macOS: Bluetooth RFCOMM transfer protocol and app-layer handshake
userStories.description: As a macOS developer, I need the RFCOMM channel to support a message protocol (JSON header + streamed content) and an app-layer handshake.
acceptanceCriteria: After RFCOMM connection, both sides exchange {"app":"CopyEverywhere","version":"3.0"} handshake; 5s timeout disconnects
Transfer protocol: sender writes JSON message header (type text/file, filename, total size) then streams content bytes
Receiver reads header, then reads exactly the declared number of bytes
Protocol works for both text and file payloads
swift build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-041 — protocol layer on top of US-041A connection

userStories.title: macOS: Bluetooth device discovery and pairing UI
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to scan for nearby Bluetooth devices running CopyEverywhere and pair with them.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigView has a 'Bluetooth Direct' section with a mode toggle (LAN Server / Bluetooth Direct)
Bluetooth mode shows 'Scan Nearby Devices' button using IOBluetoothDeviceInquiry
Scan results filtered to devices publishing CopyEverywhere RFCOMM UUID via SDP
Clicking a device triggers system-level Bluetooth pairing (macOS system dialog)
After system pairing, RFCOMM connection + app handshake runs automatically
Paired device info saved to ConfigStore; auto-reconnect on next launch
Paired device list supports 'Disconnect' and 'Forget Device' actions
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: Bluetooth mode send content
userStories.description: As a macOS user in Bluetooth mode, all send actions (drag, Cmd+V, panel send) should push content directly to the paired device via Bluetooth.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStore has transferMode property: .lanServer or .bluetooth
All send entry points (drag to menu bar, drag to panel, Cmd+V) route through ApiClient (LAN) or BluetoothService (BT) based on transferMode
Bluetooth text send: RFCOMM header + text content
Bluetooth file send: FileHandle chunk-reads streamed over RFCOMM (no full-file memory load)
Send progress exposed via AsyncStream<Double>, reuses existing progress UI
Send success/failure shows UserNotifications notification
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: Bluetooth mode receive content
userStories.description: As a macOS user, when the paired device sends content via Bluetooth, I should auto-receive and process it.
acceptanceCriteria: In Bluetooth mode, device acts as RFCOMM server listening for inbound connections
Received text is auto-written to clipboard with notification 'Received text (N chars)'
Received file is saved to Downloads folder with notification 'Received <filename>'
Receive progress shown in panel (reuses existing progress UI)
Total bytes verified against header-declared size on completion
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: Bluetooth RFCOMM service foundation (SDP + server/client connection)
userStories.description: As a Windows developer, I need a BluetoothService class using Windows.Devices.Bluetooth.Rfcomm that can publish/discover RFCOMM services and establish connections.
acceptanceCriteria: New BluetoothService class wrapping RfcommDeviceService and RfcommServiceProvider
Uses the same RFCOMM Service UUID as macOS
Server mode: RfcommServiceProvider publishes SDP record, StreamSocketListener accepts inbound connections
Client mode: DeviceInformation.FindAllAsync discovers peer service, StreamSocket connects
dotnet build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-045 — connection layer only

userStories.title: Windows: Bluetooth RFCOMM transfer protocol and app-layer handshake
userStories.description: As a Windows developer, I need the RFCOMM StreamSocket to support the same message protocol and handshake as macOS for cross-platform interop.
acceptanceCriteria: Handshake identical to macOS: exchange {"app":"CopyEverywhere","version":"3.0"}, 5s timeout
Transfer protocol identical to macOS: JSON header + streamed content
Cross-platform interop: Windows can send to macOS and vice versa
dotnet build succeeds
userStories.notes: Split from original US-045 — protocol layer on top of US-045A

userStories.title: Windows: Bluetooth device discovery and pairing UI
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to scan for nearby Bluetooth devices running CopyEverywhere and pair with them.
acceptanceCriteria: Main window config area has 'Bluetooth Direct' section with mode toggle (LAN Server / Bluetooth Direct)
Bluetooth mode shows 'Scan Nearby Devices' button using DeviceInformation.FindAllAsync + RFCOMM UUID filter
Clicking a device triggers system-level Bluetooth pairing (Windows system dialog)
After pairing, app handshake verifies peer CopyEverywhere is running
Pairing info persisted to config.json
Paired device list supports 'Disconnect' and 'Forget Device'
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: Bluetooth mode send content
userStories.description: As a Windows user in Bluetooth mode, all send actions should push content via Bluetooth to the paired device.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStore has TransferMode property: LanServer or Bluetooth
SendService dispatches to ApiClient or BluetoothService based on TransferMode
Bluetooth text and file send behavior matches macOS (RFCOMM streaming)
Send progress exposed via IProgress<double>, reuses existing progress UI
Send success/failure shows Windows Toast notification
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: Bluetooth mode receive content
userStories.description: As a Windows user, when the paired device sends content via Bluetooth, I should auto-receive and process it.
acceptanceCriteria: In Bluetooth mode, device acts as RFCOMM server listening for connections
Received text auto-written to clipboard with Toast notification
Received file saved to Downloads folder with Toast notification
Receive progress shown in main window
Byte count verification matches macOS behavior
dotnet build succeeds

userStories.title: macOS: Mode switch UI and service lifecycle
userStories.description: As a macOS user, I want to switch between LAN Server and Bluetooth Direct modes in settings, with the UI adapting to the active mode.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigView top has a Picker: 'LAN Server' / 'Bluetooth Direct'
LAN mode shows: server address (mDNS or manual), target device selector
Bluetooth mode shows: paired devices, scan button, BT connection status
Switching mode stops current background service (SSE / BT listener) and starts the new one
Panel main view hides server queue in BT mode, shows BT status and recent transfers
swift build succeeds

userStories.title: Windows: Mode switch UI and service lifecycle
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want to switch between LAN Server and Bluetooth Direct modes in settings, with the UI adapting.
acceptanceCriteria: Main window config area has ComboBox: 'LAN Server' / 'Bluetooth Direct'
LAN mode shows server address and target device selector
Bluetooth mode shows paired devices and BT connection status
Switching mode correctly stops/starts corresponding background services
Main window queue area adapts display for Bluetooth mode
dotnet build succeeds
